Employee Area

Current Open Positions at MSI

Postdoctoral / Mid Career Transition Fellows

The Center for Quantitative Genome Function (CQGF) seeks exceptional minority scientists in the fields of biology, chemistry, physics, engineering, mathematics, and computer science to work on the Alpha Project, an interdisciplinary effort in genomics. The fellowship is open to recent Ph.D. graduates and mid-career researchers seeking to transition into genomics research.

Supported by a grant awarded by the National Human Genome Research Institute (NHGRI), the Alpha Project brings together a team of investigators from various disciplines to work on an initiative that combines genomic experimentation with computation to develop models that accurately predict the behavior of biological systems. This level of biological understanding will deliver insights that can enable development of precisely targeted treatments for disease.

The core of CQGF is the Molecular Sciences Institute located in Berkeley, CA. Fellows may also conduct short term research projects in specific laboratories at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology; California Institute of Technology; and Pacific Northwest National Laboratory.

Applicants should submit a curriculum vitae, along with a cover letter explaining their background, goals, and what they expect to contribute to and learn from the Alpha Project. The applicant must send this correspondence to fellowships@molsci.org. Do not contact Dr. Brent directly. Questions can be directed to Leonore Reiser at 510-981-8738. The applicant shall also request three letters of recommendation to be sent on his/her behalf. Applicants must be U.S. citizens or permanent residents.

Address the application to:
Molecular Sciences Institute
Attn: Leonore Reiser, Ph.D.
2168 Shattuck Avenue
Floor 2
Berkeley, CA 94704
Phone 510-647-0690
Fax 510-647-0699

Or by email Leonore Reiser

Posted 11/15/2006

[ back to top ]

Technicians and Research Fellows

Effective immediately, The Molecular Sciences Institute has openings for a highly trained Technician and Research Fellow to work on a newly developed means to detect and quantify small numbers of arbitrarily designated molecules. It is likely that this work will prove useful in the detection and quantification of molecules for medical diagnoses.

Technician  requirements.
Successful experience with both recombinant DNA techniques and the expression and purification of proteins. Typically, candidates have an undergraduate degree.

Research Fellow requirements.
 Ph.D. in the life sciences and demonstrated real accomplishment during their Ph.D. work, as evidenced by significant published papers. Candidates should have previous experience in nucleic acid manipulation and protein work, and will typically have experience in protein design and engineering. The fellow will be expected to lead in the design and development of new geometries and platforms for protein-based recognition molecules, protein aptamers and other protein molecules derived from or inspired by antibodies. The fellow will lead efforts to express, select and use these new molecules.

Applicants for the Research Fellow should send a curriculum vitae along with a cover letter explaining their background, their goals, and what they expect to be able to contribute to the above work. Research fellow applicants should give names of references and cause formal letters of recommendation to be sent on their behalf. Applicants for the Technician position should include an employment history and names of three references.

Applications should be submitted to:

Human Resources
The Molecular Sciences Institute
2168 Shattuck Avenue Floor 2
Berkeley, California 94704 USA

or by email: Orna Resnekov

Posted 11/15/2006
